# GitHub Copilot Instructions

> This file provides context for AI coding agents (GitHub Copilot, Claude, etc.)
> working in the **TernTables** R package repository.

---

## 1. What TernTables Is

TernTables is a CRAN-published R package for clinical research that generates publication-ready summary tables. It automates variable-type detection, statistical test selection, and Word/Excel export so clinical researchers can produce Table 1–style outputs without a biostatistician for routine descriptive and comparative analyses.

There is a **companion web application** at [tern-tables.com](https://tern-tables.com/) that wraps this R package in a point-and-click interface. The web app is maintained in a **separate repository**. The relationship is:

- **PACKAGE** = this R package repository (`jdpreston30/TernTables`)
- **APP** = the Shiny/web application at tern-tables.com (separate repo)

The APP runs the PACKAGE under the hood. When we add features or change arguments in the PACKAGE, a corresponding issue document must be written describing what needs to change in the APP (see Section 8).

---

## 2. Repository Structure

```
TernTables/
├── R/                          # All package source code
│   ├── ternG.R                 # Grouped comparison table (largest file)
│   ├── ternD.R                 # Descriptive summary table (single cohort)
│   ├── ternB.r                 # Combines multiple tables into one Word doc
│   ├── ternP.R                 # Data preprocessing/cleaning (S3 class: ternP_result)
│   ├── ternStyle.R             # Render any custom tibble in TernTables Word style
│   ├── export_to_word.R        # Core Word rendering engine (flextable + officer)
│   ├── write_methods_doc.R     # Auto-generated statistical methods Word doc
│   ├── write_cleaning_doc.R    # Cleaning audit Word doc (for ternP output)
│   ├── utils_format.R          # Formatting helpers (val_format, val_p_format, etc.)
│   ├── utils_naming.R          # Variable name cleaning for smart_rename
│   ├── utils_posthoc.R         # CLD superscript + post-hoc logic
│   ├── utils_preprocess.R      # PHI detection + unnamed column checks
│   ├── imports.R               # Central @import / @importFrom declarations
│   ├── data.R                  # Roxygen docs for tern_colon dataset
│   └── TernTables-package.R    # Package help page + .onLoad
├── man/                        # Auto-generated .Rd files (never edit manually)
├── data/                       # tern_colon.rda
├── data-raw/                   # Scripts that build tern_colon + messy demo files
├── inst/
│   ├── CITATION                # bibentry() for citation()
│   ├── WORDLIST                # spelling exceptions for R CMD check
│   └── extdata/                # CSV/XLSX demo files for ternP examples
├── vignettes/                  # getting-started.Rmd + rendered HTML
├── development/                # NOT shipped in package (in .Rbuildignore)
│   ├── deployment-checklist.R  # CRAN submission steps
│   ├── post-cran-acceptance.md # Post-acceptance checklist
│   ├── issues/                 # Feature/issue planning docs (PACKAGE ↔ APP)
│   ├── tests/                  # Ad-hoc test scripts (not testthat)
│   ├── review/                 # Statistical review materials
│   └── tools/                  # Manual build + vignette render scripts
├── .github/
│   └── copilot-instructions.md # This file
├── DESCRIPTION
├── NAMESPACE                   # Auto-generated by roxygen2
├── NEWS.md                     # Changelog
├── README.md
├── .gitignore
└── .Rbuildignore
```

### Key architectural patterns

- **ternB_meta attribute**: `ternG()` and `ternD()` attach a `"ternB_meta"` attribute to every returned tibble. This stores the exact `word_export()` parameters used so `ternB()` can replay the rendering in a multi-table combined document. When adding a new parameter to `word_export()`, it must also be added to: the `ternB_meta` list in ternG, the `ternB_meta` list in ternD, and the replay loop in ternB.

- **Options system**: Package-wide defaults use `getOption("TernTables.<name>", <default>)` with `.onLoad` registration. Current options: `font_family`, `line_break_header`, `seed`, `vignette_outdir`.

- **All Word output flows through `word_export()`**: ternG, ternD, ternB, and ternStyle all delegate to `word_export()` for the actual flextable → officer → .docx rendering. `write_methods_doc()` and `write_cleaning_doc()` build their own officer documents directly.

---

## 3. Coding Style

### Naming conventions
- **Exported functions**: `ternG`, `ternD`, `ternB`, `ternP`, `ternStyle` (camelCase with `tern` prefix); `word_export`, `write_methods_doc`, `write_cleaning_doc`, `val_format`, `val_p_format` (snake_case)
- **Internal helpers**: Dot-prefixed (`.clean_variable_name_for_header`, `.check_phi`, `.compute_cld`)
- **Source files**: `tern*.R` for main functions, `utils_*.R` for internal helpers, `write_*.R` for doc generators
- **Note**: `ternB.r` uses lowercase `.r` (historical); all others use `.R`

### Function signatures
Parameters are typically one-per-line, left-aligned to the opening parenthesis column:
```r
ternG <- function(data,
                  vars = NULL,
                  exclude_vars = NULL,
                  group_var,
                  ...
                  font_family = getOption("TernTables.font_family", "Arial")) {
```
Some functions (notably `word_export`) use a single long line for the signature. Either style is acceptable; match the existing style in each file.

### Roxygen documentation
- One `@param` per line; multi-line descriptions indented 2 spaces
- Use `\code{}` for parameter values, function references, R expressions
- Use `\donttest{}` (not `\dontrun{}`) for examples that write files
- `@export` for public functions; `@noRd` for internal helpers
- `@seealso` to cross-reference related functions

### Code comments
- Major sections within long functions use box-drawing headers:
  ```r
  # ── Section description ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  ```
- Inline comments are plain `#` with a space

### Pipe style
- Package code uses `%>%` (magrittr) — it's an imported dependency
- `|>` (native pipe) appears in data-raw scripts and is acceptable in non-package code

### Error handling
- User-facing errors: `stop(..., call. = FALSE)`
- Informational messages: `cli::cli_alert_info()`, `cli::cli_alert_warning()`
- Never use `message()` or `cat()` for user communication

### Dependencies
- Word rendering: `officer` + `flextable`
- Data manipulation: `dplyr`, `tibble`, `stringr`
- Statistics: `stats` (base), `epitools` (OR), `rstatix` (post-hoc), `multcompView` (CLD)
- All statistical dependencies are in `Imports` (not `Suggests`)

---

## 4. Versioning & Changelog

### Version format
`major.minor.patch.dev` where `.9NNN` is the development suffix.
- Release versions: `1.6.3`
- Dev versions: `1.6.3.9015` (bumped manually, not every commit)
- The version lives in `DESCRIPTION` line 4

### NEWS.md format
```markdown
# TernTables 1.6.3.9015 (development)

## New features

* **`feature_name` parameter**: Description of what it does and why.

## Bug fixes

* **Root cause description**: What went wrong and how it was fixed.
  Technical detail if relevant.

## Internal changes

* Refactored X to improve Y.

---
```
Entries are detailed — bug fixes include root cause, not just "fixed X".

---

## 5. Deployment Checklist

The canonical deployment sequence lives in `development/deployment-checklist.R`:
1. `devtools::document()` — regenerates NAMESPACE + man/ pages
2. `source("development/tools/manual/build-manual.R")` — builds PDF manual
3. `rmarkdown::render("vignettes/getting-started.Rmd")` — renders vignette
4. `devtools::check()` — full R CMD check (target: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes)
5. For CRAN: `devtools::check_win_devel()` → `devtools::submit_cran()`

---

## 6. Adding a New Parameter (full wiring checklist)

When adding a new argument that affects Word output, wire it through ALL of these:

1. **`export_to_word.R`** — add to `word_export()` signature + roxygen + function body
2. **`ternG.R`** — add to signature, roxygen, `word_export()` call, and `ternB_meta` list
3. **`ternD.R`** — add to signature, roxygen, `word_export()` call, and `ternB_meta` list
4. **`ternB.r`** — add to signature, roxygen, and the replay `word_export()` call inside the loop (use the `ternB()` argument, **not** `meta$<param>`, if you want a single value to apply across all tables)
5. **`ternStyle.R`** — add to signature and the delegating `word_export()` call
6. **`write_methods_doc.R`** and/or **`write_cleaning_doc.R`** — if the parameter affects companion documents, add there too

If the parameter has a package-wide default, register it in `.onLoad()` in `TernTables-package.R` using the `getOption("TernTables.<name>", <default>)` pattern.

---

## 7. Commit Message Convention

When asked to "commit" or generate a commit message, produce a **copy-pasteable** message in this format:

```
v<VERSION>: Concise summary of all changes

- Bullet point for each distinct change
- Group related changes together
- Use present tense ("Add", "Fix", "Wire", not "Added", "Fixed")
```

The version comes from `DESCRIPTION` line 4. Example:
```
v1.6.3.9015: Add font_family parameter, fix footnote spacing, fix plain_header underline with variable_footnote

- Add font_family parameter to word_export, ternG, ternD, ternB, ternStyle, write_methods_doc, write_cleaning_doc
- Replace all 27 hardcoded "Arial" references with font_family variable
- Default: getOption("TernTables.font_family", "Arial")
- Fix footnote section spacing: separate abbreviations, CLD note, symbol definitions, and table_footnote with blank lines
- Fix plain_header underline being dropped when row also has a variable_footnote symbol (compose() was overwriting fp_text properties)
```

---

## 8. PACKAGE ↔ APP Issue Documents

Every **major PACKAGE change** that affects user-facing functionality (new arguments, changed defaults, new functions) requires an issue document written to `development/issues/`. The document must clearly separate:

### PACKAGE changes (what changed here in the R package)
- Which functions gained new parameters
- What the parameter does, its type, and default value
- Any changed behavior or new defaults

### APP changes (what probably needs to change in the web app)
- Where in the APP UI the new feature should surface
- What control type to use (dropdown, checkbox, text input, etc.)
- What the default should be
- How the selected value maps to the R function argument
- Example backend R call showing the new argument in context

### Issue file format

```markdown
# Feature: <short name>

## What changed in the package
- Which function(s) were modified
- New argument name(s), type(s), default(s)
- What the argument controls
- Any changes to output structure or side effects

## Web app integration required
### Likely UI changes
- Which Step 2 sub-component(s) need a new control
- Where in the Options panel it should appear (base vs Advanced)
- Whether it warrants a new sub-step

### Plumber API changes (R/plumber.R)
- Which endpoint(s) need the new param read from `req$body`
- Whether it needs to be passed to the preview citation-free call as well
- If it should appear in the generated `_reproduce.R` script (rscript.R)

### Frontend API changes (client/src/api/client.js)
- New field(s) to add to the POST body in `analyzeData()` or similar

### State changes (App.jsx / Step2.jsx)
- New state variable(s) needed and where they live

## Notes / open questions
- Edge cases, interactions with existing arguments
- Whether the argument should be "always on" (hardcoded) or user-exposed
```

### File naming
`development/issues/<feature-name>.md` — e.g., `feature-font-family.md`, `feature-plain-header.md`

These docs are committed to the repo (inside `development/`, which is excluded from the R package build via `.Rbuildignore`).

---

## 9. The Two-Repo System

There are two repositories that work together:

| Repo | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `jdpreston30/TernTables` | The R package — `ternP()`, `ternG()`, `ternD()`, `ternB()`, `ternStyle()` |
| `jdpreston30/TernTables-service` | The web app — Plumber API + React frontend at tern-tables.com |

**You are working in the PACKAGE repo.** Changes you make here — new arguments, changed defaults, new functions, changed output structures — all have potential downstream effects on the APP. Your job includes anticipating those effects and documenting them in the issue files (Section 8).

### Arguments the APP hardcodes (do not change behavior without flagging)
- `open_doc = FALSE` — the web app never opens files; users running locally expect `TRUE` (the package default)
- `citation = FALSE` for preview rendering, `TRUE` for download — don't remove this parameter
- `smart_rename = FALSE` — the APP handles renaming upstream via its own AI-powered UI step
- `factor_order = "mixed"` — always passed by the APP
- `insert_subheads = TRUE` — hardcoded for ternD

---

## 10. Web App Workflow (for PACKAGE → APP issue planning)

The web application at [tern-tables.com](https://tern-tables.com/) walks users through a 3-step linear workflow:

### Step 1 — Upload & Preprocess
- User provides a table title and uploads `.xlsx` or `.csv`
- The service calls `ternP()` and shows feedback (columns dropped, rows flagged, type changes)
- User sees a "Cleaning Summary" and can download sparse row info

### Step 2 — Configuration (multi-sub-step)
1. **Variable Renaming** — AI-powered (Gemini) suggestion of human-readable column names; user can override. This runs *before* passing column names to `ternG()`, so `smart_rename = FALSE` is always passed.
2. **Analysis Type** — Grouped comparison (→ `ternG`) or Descriptive only (→ `ternD`)
3. **Variable Selection** — User picks which columns to include (`vars`)
4. **Normality Preview** — Shows `classify_normality()` results so user understands what's being treated as normal vs non-normal
5. **Variable Ordering / Factor Ordering** — Drag-and-drop. Sets `category_start` (section headers), `group_order` (column order within grouping var), and `binary_ref` (reference level for OR)
6. **Options** — Font size, font family, caption, footnote, OR column, normality handling override, FDR correction (`p_adjust`, `p_adjust_display`), show test column, show normality column

### Step 3 — Download Outputs
- Table preview rendered as PNG (citation-free `_preview.docx` → PDF → PNG via LibreOffice + magick)
- Download buttons: Word table (`.docx`), PDF, Excel, cleaned CSV, R script, cleaning summary, statistical methods doc
- Multi-table session: user can run another analysis and bundle all tables into one Word doc via `ternB()`

---

## 11. Web App Architecture Map

```
Browser
│
├── App.jsx                   Top-level state machine
│   ├── sessionId             UUID per upload; all API calls carry this
│   ├── currentStep           1 | 2 | 3
│   └── analysisResult        what ternG/ternD returned
│
├── Step1/
│   ├── UploadPanel.jsx       File drag-drop + table title input
│   └── FeedbackPanel.jsx     ternP feedback display
│
├── Step2/
│   ├── AnalysisType.jsx      "Grouped" vs "Descriptive" choice
│   ├── VariableRenaming.jsx  AI rename UI; produces display_name per column
│   ├── VariableSelection.jsx Checkbox list → builds vars[]
│   ├── NormalityPreview.jsx  classify_normality() results table
│   ├── VariableOrdering.jsx  Drag-drop; builds category_start, group_order
│   ├── GroupingVariable.jsx  Picks group_var
│   ├── GroupOrdering.jsx     Sets group_order + binary_ref
│   ├── Options.jsx           All ternG() flags (grouped analysis)
│   └── OptionsDescriptive.jsx  ternD() flags (descriptive analysis)
│
├── Step3/
│   └── Step3.jsx             Preview + download buttons + bundle
│
├── common/
│   ├── StepIndicator.jsx     3-step progress pill
│   ├── SortableItem.jsx      @dnd-kit drag item wrapper
│   ├── RCodePanel.jsx        Syntax-highlighted R code display
│   └── FloatingActions.jsx   Sticky bottom action bar
│
└── api/client.js             All fetch() calls to Plumber API
```

```
R/plumber.R                   All API routes (~1300 lines)
│
├── POST /api/upload          → ternP(), saves session
├── POST /api/classify-normality  → classify_normality()
├── POST /api/suggest-headers → Gemini API (AI rename)
├── POST /api/analyze         → ternG() + citation-free ternG() for preview
├── POST /api/analyze-descriptive → ternD() + citation-free ternD() for preview
├── POST /api/download/bundle → ternB()
├── GET  /api/download/:type  → streams docx/pdf/xlsx/csv/methods/cleaning/rscript/zip
├── GET  /api/preview-png     → LibreOffice + magick → PNG
└── GET  /api/health, /api/stats, /api/version

R/helpers/
├── session.R                 UUID session dir management (saveRDS/readRDS)
├── classify.R                classify_normality() — used for NormalityPreview step
├── feedback.R                ternP feedback message generation
└── rscript.R                 generate_r_script() — builds the downloadable _reproduce.R
```

---

## 12. Package Argument → Web UI Mapping

This table tracks which `ternG()` / `ternD()` arguments are currently surfaced in the web app:

| Argument | In UI? | Where |
|---|---|---|
| `vars` | ✅ | Variable Selection step |
| `group_var` | ✅ | Grouping Variable step |
| `group_order` | ✅ | Group Ordering drag-drop |
| `category_start` | ✅ | Variable Ordering drag-drop (section headers) |
| `OR_col` | ✅ | Options → Advanced (hidden for >2 groups) |
| `binary_ref` | ✅ | Group Ordering (reference level selector) |
| `force_ordinal` | ✅ | Variable Ordering (toggle per variable) |
| `consider_normality` | ✅ | Options → Advanced (4-way radio) |
| `show_test` | ✅ | Options → Advanced |
| `print_normality` | ✅ | Options → Advanced |
| `p_adjust` | ✅ | Options → base section |
| `p_adjust_display` | ✅ | Options → base section (conditional radio) |
| `round_intg` | ✅ | Options → base |
| `table_font_size` | ✅ | Options → base |
| `table_caption` | ✅ | Options → base |
| `table_footnote` | ✅ | Options → base |
| `font_family` | 🔜 | Options → base (dropdown, see `development/issues/feature-font-family.md`) |
| `factor_order` | ❌ | Hardcoded to `"mixed"` |
| `smart_rename` | ❌ | Hardcoded to `FALSE` (done upstream) |
| `open_doc` | ❌ | Hardcoded to `FALSE` |
| `citation` | ❌ | `FALSE` for preview, `TRUE` for download |
| `insert_subheads` | ❌ | Hardcoded to `TRUE` (ternD only) |
| `methods_doc` | ❌ | Hardcoded to `TRUE` for main run, `FALSE` for preview |
| `output_xlsx` / `output_docx` | ❌ | Constructed from `table_title` |

When adding a new argument, update this table and note whether it should be user-exposed (UI control) or hardcoded.

---

## 13. The Reproducible R Script

Every analysis the APP generates produces a `_reproduce.R` file (via `generate_r_script()` in `R/helpers/rscript.R` of the service repo). This script contains every argument that was passed to `ternG()` or `ternD()` that differs from the function's default, so a user can reproduce the exact table locally.

When you add a new argument to `ternG()`/`ternD()`, the issue file **must** note whether and how the argument should appear in the generated script.

---

## 14. Deployment Notes (context only)

The service runs on AWS Lightsail (`tern-tables.com`). Package updates only take effect on the server after installing via `remotes::install_github` and restarting the service. Merging to `main` in the package repo does not auto-deploy anything.

**Standard server deploy flow** (use the `deploy-pkg` shortcut):
1. `git push origin main` — local
2. `sudo Rscript -e "remotes::install_github('jdpreston30/TernTables', force = TRUE)"` — server
3. `sudo systemctl restart terntables-r` — server

SSH key: `~/.ssh/LightsailDefaultKey-us-east-1-1.pem`, user: `ubuntu@tern-tables.com`
Service name: `terntables-r`

---

## 15. Shortcut Commands

When the user sends one of these short messages, execute the corresponding action immediately without asking for confirmation.

### `deploy`
Run the full deployment checklist **in sequence** in an R terminal. Each command must finish before the next starts. Stop and report if any command errors:
```r
devtools::document()
source("development/tools/manual/build-manual.R")
source("development/tools/run-vignette/run-vignette.R")
rmarkdown::render("vignettes/getting-started.Rmd")
devtools::check()
```

### `commit`
Generate a copy-pasteable commit message **in the chat** using the format in Section 7. Read the current version from `DESCRIPTION` line 4, diff the working tree, and produce the message. **Do not** create any files. **Do not** run `git commit` or any git commands. Just output the formatted message text so the user can copy-paste it.

### `wire <param>`
Walk through the full parameter wiring checklist (Section 6) for the named parameter. Confirm the parameter's type, default, and purpose, then systematically add it to all required files: `word_export()`, `ternG()`, `ternD()`, `ternB()`, `ternStyle()`, and companion doc functions as applicable.

### `issue <feature-name>`
Create a new issue document at `development/issues/<feature-name>.md` using the template in Section 8. Pre-fill the PACKAGE changes section based on recent work, leave APP changes sections as prompts to fill in.

### `deploy-pkg`
Push the current local package to GitHub, then install it on the server and restart the service. Run these steps **in sequence**, stopping and reporting if any step errors:

1. `git push origin main` — push local commits to GitHub
2. SSH to server and run:
   ```bash
   sudo Rscript -e "remotes::install_github('jdpreston30/TernTables', force = TRUE)"
   sudo systemctl restart terntables-r
   sudo systemctl status terntables-r --no-pager | head -6
   ```

SSH key: `~/.ssh/LightsailDefaultKey-us-east-1-1.pem`
Server: `ubuntu@tern-tables.com`

> **Note:** This is the standard server deploy flow. Do NOT use `R CMD build` + `scp` + `R CMD INSTALL` unless `remotes::install_github` fails.

---

## 16. Auto-Prompt for APP Issue Documents

After completing a PACKAGE change, evaluate whether it affects the web app. **Ask the user** before creating anything — never auto-generate issue files silently.

### Changes that SHOULD trigger an issue prompt
These are app-impacting changes. After completing the work, ask: *"This change affects the web app. Want me to create an issue document?"*

- New exported function or parameter added to `ternG()`, `ternD()`, `ternB()`, `ternStyle()`, or `word_export()`
- Changed default value of any existing parameter
- Changed output structure (new columns, renamed columns, different tibble shape)
- New or changed footnote behavior visible in Word output
- New `getOption("TernTables.*")` option registered
- Anything that changes what the Plumber API would need to pass or how the frontend would need to render results

### Changes that should NOT trigger an issue prompt
These are internal or silent — the web app doesn't need to know about them:

- Bug fixes that don't change the function signature or output structure
- Internal helper refactors (`.dot_prefixed` functions)
- Documentation-only changes (roxygen, vignettes, README)
- Test scripts in `development/tests/`
- Code style cleanups, comment updates
- CRAN compliance fixes (NAMESPACE, .Rbuildignore, WORDLIST)
- Changes to `ternP()` internals (the APP already calls it — unless the return structure changes)

### Prompt behavior
- **One prompt per logical feature**, not per file edited. If wiring a parameter touches 6 files, that's one issue document, not six.
- If the user declines, move on — don't ask again for the same change.
- If the user says `issue <name>` later, create it then.

---

## 17. Files You Should Never Edit Manually

- `man/*.Rd` — auto-generated by `devtools::document()` from roxygen
- `NAMESPACE` — auto-generated by `devtools::document()` from `@export` / `@import` tags
- `data/tern_colon.rda` — regenerated by `data-raw/tern_colon.R`
- `inst/extdata/**` — regenerated by `data-raw/tern_colon_extdata.R`
